title: Registration Open for 2024 LA County Science Fair
excerpt: We are thrilled to announce that registration for the 74th Annual LA County Science & Engineering Fair is now officially open. Students, teachers, and judges can begin the onboarding process.

title: New Rubric Guidelines Released
excerpt: The judging committee has updated the evaluation rubrics for the Senior Division engineering categories to better reflect modern prototyping standards.

title: Alumni Spotlight: Sarah Chen's Water Filtration Journey
excerpt: Read about how last year's grand prize winner took her low-cost water filtration project from the LA Fair to an international symposium.

title: Webinar: Supporting Student Research
excerpt: Join our upcoming webinar next Thursday where experienced faculty will share strategies for guiding students through long-term scientific research projects.

title: How to Format Your Abstract
excerpt: A step-by-step guide on writing a clear, concise, and compelling abstract for your project submission. Includes examples of strong abstracts from past fairs.

title: Judge Orientation Session Scheduled for February 28
excerpt: All judges are invited to attend a virtual orientation on February 28 covering the updated rubrics, conflict-of-interest policy, and day-of logistics.

title: Project Safety Checklist for Lab Research
excerpt: Before you run your first experiment, review our comprehensive safety checklist. Hazardous materials, animal studies, and human subjects research all require prior approval.

title: Call for Fair Volunteers — Help Make the Magic Happen
excerpt: We need 80+ volunteers on Fair Day to keep things running smoothly. Sign up to help with check-in, setup, awards, or student support — no science background required.

title: Display Board Design Tips from Past Winners
excerpt: Your display board is a visual argument for your research. We asked three grand-prize alumni what they wish they had known when designing theirs.

title: Junior Division: What 6th–8th Graders Can Expect
excerpt: New to the fair? The Junior Division offers a welcoming, low-pressure environment. Here is everything a middle-schooler needs to know before Fair Day.

title: Interview Skills Workshop — January Recap
excerpt: Over 120 students attended our January workshop on presenting research to judges. Watch the recording and download the slide deck for practice questions.

title: Mentor Match Program Now Open for 2024
excerpt: Students can now request a volunteer STEM mentor to help guide their projects. Mentors commit to two one-hour check-ins before Fair Day.

title: LACSEF Alumni Earns Full Caltech Scholarship
excerpt: Marcus Delgado, a 2022 Senior Division grand winner, credits his microbiology fair project with landing a full-ride scholarship to Caltech. He shares what the experience taught him.

title: 2024 Project Category Descriptions Released
excerpt: From behavioral science to environmental engineering, all 16 competition categories for 2024 are now described in detail. Make sure you choose the one that best fits your project.

title: Teacher Stipend Program Returns for 2024
excerpt: Teachers who sponsor three or more student projects can apply for a $500 classroom stipend. Applications open January 15; funding is limited to 30 educators.

title: 2023 Fair Recap: Highlights from 400 Projects
excerpt: More than 400 projects competed this year across 16 categories. Relive the best moments, see the full award list, and read about the projects that earned state-fair bids.

title: New Data Science & AI Category Added for 2024
excerpt: Responding to growing student interest, LACSEF is adding a dedicated Data Science & Artificial Intelligence category for 2024. Projects must include an original dataset and analysis.

title: Parent & Guardian FAQ
excerpt: How much can a parent help? What does it cost? Where do we go on Fair Day? We answer the 15 questions parents ask us most every year.

title: Congratulations to Our 2023 ISEF Qualifiers
excerpt: Eight LACSEF students have earned spots at the 2024 International Science and Engineering Fair. Meet the qualifiers and learn about their winning projects.

title: Keeping a Research Lab Notebook — A Student Guide
excerpt: Your lab notebook is the legal record of your original work. Learn what judges look for, what to write after every session, and how to handle mistakes the right way.

title: 10 New Regional Partner Schools Announced
excerpt: LACSEF has partnered with 10 additional schools across the Antelope Valley and South Bay regions, expanding fair access to underrepresented communities.

title: Recruiting STEM Professionals to Judge
excerpt: We need 200 judges with STEM backgrounds for Fair Day 2024. It is a single morning commitment and one of the most rewarding ways to invest in the next generation of scientists.

title: Research Ethics: A Primer for Young Scientists
excerpt: Plagiarism, data fabrication, and conflicts of interest can disqualify a project. This guide explains the ethical standards every LACSEF student must follow.

title: 50 Years of Grand Award Winners: A Look Back
excerpt: From a 1973 soil erosion study to last year's AI-assisted cancer screening model, trace how student research has mirrored the biggest scientific challenges of each era.

title: LACSEF Receives $250,000 Outreach Grant
excerpt: A new grant from the Herrera Family Foundation will fund science mentorship kits, transportation subsidies, and teacher training in underserved LA County schools through 2026.

title: LACSEF Launches new website
excerpt: New website launched
